starter_prompt_text:
Write a tentative answer, then send one reviewer child a focused request to critique it, verify it, or improve it before the final answer is produced.
The reviewer child should help stress-test the draft, not wander into unrelated subproblems.
Use plain text only inside the draft and reviewer message. Do not use markdown, code fences, or LaTeX-style backslashes.
Keep the JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Make draft_response exactly two short lines:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Keep message_for_reviewer to two short sentences or fewer.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "review",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "draft_response": "plain-text draft answer",
  "message_for_reviewer": "plain-text review request for the child"
}
execute_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}
current_prompt_text:
Perform a two-stage process: 1) Conduct a data sufficiency audit to verify if all necessary parameters, target values, and cost functions are present in the problem statement. 2) If and only if the data is complete, develop a rigorous, step-by-step algorithmic strategy or mathematical derivation to solve the optimization problem. If data is missing, your draft must clearly identify the specific missing components. 

Once the draft is prepared, delegate to a reviewer child to perform a critical audit. The reviewer must specifically check for: 1) accuracy of the data sufficiency assessment, 2) logical soundness of the proposed algorithm, and 3) identification of any missed constraints.

Use plain text only inside the draft and reviewer message. Do not use markdown, code fences, or LaTeX-style backslashes.
Keep the JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short (3 to 8 words).
Make draft_response exactly two short lines:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Keep message_for_reviewer to two short sentences or fewer, targeting a specific part of the audit or derivation.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

  "draft_response": "plain-text draft answer",
  "message_for_reviewer": "plain-text review request for the child"
}
